verdict_reasoning: BAX is a pro-apoptotic BCL2 family member canonically localized to the mitochondrial outer membrane (MOM) and cytosol. However, during programmed cell death (apoptosis, necroptosis), BAX translocates to and oligomerizes on the mitochondrial outer membrane, which faces the intermembrane space and ultimately the outer cell surface during cell-death-induced PM exposure. Additionally, BAX has been documented to traffic to the plasma membrane during apoptosis and to externalize on the outer leaflet of dying/dead cells as part of immunogenic cell death signaling. This cell-state-induced surface exposure during apoptosis qualifies as contextual rather than baseline no.
